title: Spatially resolved transcriptomic profiling of human healthy gastric corpus glands using NanoString GeoMx DSP
author: Boccellato, Francesco
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Experimental design 
 Spatial transcriptomic profiling was performed on healthy human gastric corpus tissue from three donors using the NanoString GeoMx DSP Whole Transcriptome Atlas (WTA). Tissue sections were segmented into five anatomical regions: foveola, isthmus, neck, base, and muscularis mucosae. Within each region, epithelial and stromal compartments were separated using morphology marker-based segmentation (E-cadherin and Vimentin). Segments corresponding to regions 1–4 include both epithelial and stromal compartments, while region 5 (muscularis mucosae) contains stromal cells only.

Experimental design 
 Spatial transcriptomic profiling was performed on healthy human gastric corpus tissue from three donors using the NanoString GeoMx DSP Whole Transcriptome Atlas (WTA). Tissue sections were segmented into five anatomical regions: foveola, isthmus, neck, base, and muscularis mucosae. Within each region, epithelial and stromal compartments were separated using morphology marker-based segmentation (E-cadherin and Vimentin). Segments corresponding to regions 1–4 include both epithelial and stromal compartments, while region 5 (muscularis mucosae) contains stromal cells only.
